revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Chasuriyama Kofun]][[ja:茶すり山古墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Chasuriyama Kofun | native_name =茶すり山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 茶すり山古墳出土 銅鏡・鉄製品.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Artifacts recovered from the Chasuriyama Kofun (ICP) | map_type = Japan Hyōgo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Chasuriyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.309555555555555|N|134.86119444444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Asago|qid=Q720402|1=Asago, Hyōgo}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park and museum) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|circular kofun|qid=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]|qq=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|18|34.4|N|134|51|40.3|E|qq=}}|qq=}}|qq=}} The {{nihongo|'''Chasuriyama Kofun'''|茶すり山古墳||qq=}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Ikunocho Kuchiganaya neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Asago, Hyōgo|en|Asago, Hyōgo|qid=Q720402|lt=Asago, Hyōgo|1=Asago, Hyōgo}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2004.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/140224 |title=茶すり山古墳 |trans-title= Chasuriyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021|qq=}}</ref> ==Overview== The Chasuriyama Kofun is located at the tip of a narrow ridge overlooking the Wadayama basin in the southern part of the {{ill|Tajima Province|en|Tajima Province|qid=Q1053727|lt=Tajima region|1=Tajima Province}}. This area is a transportation hub that connects {{ill|Harima Province|en|Harima Province|qid=Q1047523|lt=Harima|1=Harima Province}} and Tajima, and is on the estimated route of the ancient San-in highway from the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]] to the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|qid=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan|1=Sea of Japan}} and the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|qid=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} of Japan. It is a large {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=}}-style elliptical Kofun with a major axis of 90 meters, minor axis of 78 meters and a height of 18 meters. The mound was considerably damaged by the construction of a fortification in the 16th century. Part of the ''[[fukiishi]]'' remain in the northeastern quadrant mound, and fragments of both cylindrical and morning glory-shaped ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found. However, some of the ''haniwa'' shards remained in situ and could be completely restored. The construction period of the tumulus is estimated to be from the first half of the 5th century from the style of ''haniwa'' used.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=}}{{in lang|ja|qq=}}</ref> The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 2001in conjunction with construction of the {{ill|Kitakinki-Toyooka Expressway|en|Kitakinki-Toyooka Expressway|qid=Q6157667|lt=Kitakinki-Toyooka Expressway|1=Kitakinki-Toyooka Expressway}}, during which time two [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s, each containing multiple burials, and a very large quantity of {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} were discovered. Due to the very large quantity of weapons, it is assumed that the persons buried in this tumulus were warrior chieftains.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The first chamber was 13.6 by 10.1 meters and contained a long clay-covered wooden casket orientated to the each. Inside, the casket was painted red and was divided in several sections by partitions. The main (central) partition contained the burial, with side-rooms to the east and west, which were further sub-divided. Figurative ''haniwa'', one of which depicted a house, were also found within the casket. Grave goods included three {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|qid=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s, (one of which appears to be [[Han dynasty#Eastern Han|Late Han dynasty]]), two sets of armor, and 10 iron swords, hatchets and other bladed weapons and 370 iron arrowheads, remnants of leather shields with rhombus or sawtooth decorations, ''[[magatama]]'' and glass beads. The second burial chamber was smaller, at 7.5 by 3.7 meters, and the wooden casket had no clay covering. As with the first burial chamber, the casket was divided into compartments; however, the number of grave goods was smaller and different in nature. One bronze mirror was placed on the chest of the deceased, who also a necklace with ''magatama'' and other beads, and two iron swords were found, one on either side of the body. The eastern sub-chamber had more than 50 iron agricultural implements, but these were too small to have been of practical use and appear to have been made as ritual objects. The western sub-chamber had on 14 iron arrowheads.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The artifacts were collectively designated a National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} in 2013.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/292449 |title=兵庫県茶すり山古墳出土品 |trans-title= Hyōgo ken chasuriyama kofun shutsudohin|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021|qq=}}</ref> Currently, the site is preserved as is the Chasuriyama Kofun Park, with some of the artifacts on display at the {{nihongo|'''Chasuriyama Kofun Learning Center'''|茶すり山古墳学習館|Chasuriyama kofun gakushū-kan|qq=}} on site. It is located about 18 minutes by car from {{ill|Yanase Station|en|Yanase Station|qid=Q870907|lt=Yanase Station|1=Yanase Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|qid=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line|1=San'in Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/> ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)|qid=Q104629006|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)}} ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} ==External links== *[https://www.asabura.jp/sspot/828 Asago City Portal] {{in lang|ja|qq=}} *[https://www.city.asago.hyogo.jp/0000000417.html Asago City home page] {{in lang|ja|qq=}} *[http://asago-kanko.com/spot/9642 Asago CityTourist Information] {{in lang|ja|qq=}}
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
{{draft categories||qq=}}
